title: selago scabribractea
intro: Selago scabribractea, also known as the hairy-leaved selago, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to South Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
